senses.gloss: July (Solar calendar used in Palestine, Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, and Iraq)

etymology.text: From Classical Syriac ܬܡܘܙ (tāmūz), ultimately from Akkadian 𒌚𒋗 (dumuzu). Cognate with Hebrew תַּמּוּז (tammuz, “Tammuz, the fourth month of the Hebrew lunisolar calendar”).

body.text: to the god of that name,

entries.lexicon_en: Al-Tahānawī, Kashshāf Iṣṭilāḥāt al-Funūn wa-l-ʿUlūm (d. 1777 CE)
entries.body_html:
تموز:
[في الانكليزية] July
[ في الفرنسية] Juillet
اسم شهر في التقويم الرومي.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)
entries.lexicon_ar: المعجم العربي الإنجليزي لإدوارد وليام لين
entries.body_html:
تموز



تَمُّوزُ [sometimes written تَمُوزُ, without tesh-deed,] The [Syrian] month [sacred, in ancient times, to the god of that name, (mentioned in Ezek. viii. 14,) corresponding to July, O. S.,] after حَزِيرانُ. (S in art. حزر.)
